rows.label: Stage-B self-hosting — WASM execution (P9)
rows.status: in progress — 3 of 7 stages RUN · ★ 4 of 7 carry #100 (3 TRAP at COMPILE, 1 at EXECUTION) — lexer, parser + gir-emitter run; erasure ⟺ trap, exact across all seven, and gir-emitter is now the PROOF of it: un-erase its AST params (Array<Auto> → the parser's FlowDecl/Expr/Stmt) and it runs (2026-07-19, P9 brick-2). PROVEN at R3 (byte-parity ≡ Stage-A through real WASM behind #105): lexer `tokenize` · the whole PARSER ladder to its entry point — parseParams (flat) → parseExpr (recursive) → parseStmt (self+mutual) → parseBlock → parseFlows (2026-07-17, 53 differential tests, all first-run green); the recursive AST readback needed NO new ABI (that was an assertion; it is now executed) · gir-emitter `emitGIRModule` (2026-07-19, Stage-A interpreter == Stage-B WASM over a parsed corpus). ★ TRAP AT R2 (compile) — type-checker · effect-checker · governance-verifier: each is R0-clean, R0-assembles, R1-#105-admits AND EXPORTS, then throws `unreachable` on one trivial flow. gir-emitter was the FIRST of the #100 stages UN-ERASED (2026-07-19, P9 brick-2 via Option Y — concatenate the real lexer+parser so the parser's FlowDecl/Expr/Stmt records are in scope, then type emitGIRModule's `flows` param + its callees' AST params off Array<Auto>; a typed local `let re: ReturnExpr = fd.returnExpr` clears the scalar-record-field erasure too): it now RUNS, audit-stage-execution.mjs baseline 4→3, emitGIRModule R3 byte-parity green. ALSO carries #100 but traps at EXECUTION not compile: runtime `envLookup` reads b.name/b.val off an Array<Auto> payload, reached by every variable reference (runProgram→evalGIRExpr→envLookup) — construct verified in source, not yet driven. ROOT CAUSE (isolated one-variable-at-a-time, 20-line repro with R&D): reading a RECORD FIELD off an Option payload obtained from `.get()` on an `Array<Auto>` — Array<Auto> alone is fine, get+field on a CONCRETE array is fine, the two TOGETHER erase the element type so the field offset is unknown at lowering and the emitter falls through to `unreachable`. That is TASK #100, now P9's blocker; the stages take Array<Auto> per their own headers 'to avoid generic syntax constraints in Stage B param declarations' — the workaround IS the bug. THE FIX (R&D ruling 2026-07-17, verified in emitter source): the wat-emitter.ts:1340 trap is DELIBERATE fail-closed (#163) — do NOT touch it; only REAL Stage-B generics (un-erase the element type end-to-end, INCLUDING the get→Option→match-bind inference chain) unblocks R2 (does-it-RUN) for the #100 stages (5 originally; gir-emitter cleared first 2026-07-19, 4 to go) — NOT R3. Option/enum-variant lowering (the fix that fits #100's NAME) does NOT unblock it, because the discriminator is the erasure, NOT the Option — the R2 control case proves it by execution: the identical body over a CONCRETE array RUNS. Patch-per-site is visibly wrong; un-erasure at source clears each stage AT R2 — gir-emitter first (2026-07-19, DONE), the remaining three (type/effect/gov-verifier) next. ★ R3 (byte-parity) is a LARGER, DISTINCT track (R&D correction 2026-07-17): 'fix #100 → self-hosting' is true for does-it-RUN, false for does-it-byte-MATCH — R3 needs the WAT emitter's TOTAL un-lowered-node debt → 0 (385 module-wide nodes across the 7 stages, measured by audit-unlowered-nodes.mjs, ALL #128; #100's field-reads are an INDISTINGUISHABLE subset). The emitter is a PARTIAL emitter; #100 is ONE class of its completeness gap, not the whole. Fixing #100 buys R2-green on those stages, NOT R3. ALSO: the stages cannot be concatenated — `appendAll` is defined in both type-checker and governance-verifier (`paramNames` in type-checker and gir-emitter), the compiler accepts duplicate FLOW names (FUNGI-NAME-002 covers bindings only), and it surfaces only as a WASM `Duplicate export name` at instantiate — so the whole-compiler bundle P9 ends at cannot be built today. ⚠ THE ROW WAS THE LESSON: 'all 7 twins build (R0) + admit (R1), flows callable' stood from 2026-07-12 and read as 'the stages work'. They ARE callable. THREE now trap at compile-time (gir-emitter un-erased 2026-07-19, the first flip), a fourth (runtime) at execution. R1 asks 'does it export?', R3 asks 'does it agree?' — nothing asked 'does it RUN?' (that is R2, now built: audit-stage-execution.mjs). UNSWEPT (declared): runtime — execution-time #100 casualty, a different surface from this compile-pipeline sweep (same root, same un-erasure fix).

RESOLVED 2026-07-16 — the hybrid root 21415420b447e219 moved OFF the plaintext env-file into an OFFLINE secret store (owner); custody README corrected (it had mis-named the key + a dangerous restore step) + root-vs-operational split plan written (answers #72 — the registry authority is a NEW operational key, never the cold root). The 'wipe the 2 remaining plaintext copies' residual is CLOSED (2026-07-18 custody close-out, re-verified 2026-07-19: key-vault empty, GitHub\keys absent, every .pem on disk is a .pub half; the only private key present is the repo-root .env.galerina-signing = 53de6be4, gitignored, which belongs there). OPEN instead: (a) the offline root has ONE copy — duplicate to 2-3 media with destination verification, the exact ab46f4c7 machine-rebuild risk; (b) NOT a residual — recorded so it stops being re-raised: per-machine dev keys auto-mint into a package's governance/ dir when a build runs with cwd inside it (api-protocol-rest's own package.json build script does). That is BY DESIGN — .gitignore:34 ignores **/governance/signing-key-*.pub.pem with :39 negating the single ceremony anchor 21415420, so minted pubkeys are build artifacts, never governance records, and key-inventory is CORRECT to omit them. Owner mints deliberately. Do not flag, do not 'delete on sight'. key-rotation itself SHIPS (tower-citizen/key-rotation.ts). TPM(L3)/HW(L4) + HSM/PKCS#11 = post-v1 hardening
